evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix fully implemented: diagonal annotated with values on dark navy, off-diagonal scaled with Blues sequential cmap — brief's claim is clearly carried.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF uses Type3 (outlined) fonts and SVG has 0 <text> elements (all paths) — text is not selectable/editable; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blues sequential colormap matches brief suggestion and is reasonably colorblind-safe; diagonal uses distinct dark navy (#1a5276); no problematic red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 8x7 inch figure with tight_layout and colorbar; cell annotations at 8-10pt; grid lines at 0.4lw; layout appears sensible but font sizes are small for a dense 10x10 grid.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure with correct color encoding details; colorbar is labeled; per-class accuracy range stated; no legend needed beyond colorbar.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists (26 KB) but fonts are Type3 (outlined glyphs, not selectable text) because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set; SVG similarly has all text as path outlines.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (68 KB), caption.tex (510 B), gen_figure.py (2.5 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains row/column semantics, color encoding for both diagonal and off-diagonal, and provides a specific numerical finding: per-class accuracy 69% (C1) to 92% (C8).

overall_strengths: Fully satisfies the brief: 10x10 confusion matrix with diagonal annotation and sequential Blues colormap for off-diagonal cells, with adaptive text color for readability.
Caption is publication-quality with clear encoding description and a concrete numerical claim (69%–92% accuracy range).
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none': both PDF and SVG embed text as outlined paths (Type3/path elements), making text non-selectable and preventing journal copy-editing.
Uses matplotlib default DejaVu Sans with no explicit font configuration; a publication font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif) should be specified explicitly.
